dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal uses momentum reversal as dynamic exit mechanism. MACD crossovers proxy for momentum direction reversals. Entry conditions are directional (LONG or SHORT based on MACD crossing above/below signal line). Exit conditions automatically trigger on opposite crossover, implementing the 'reversal signal as dynamic stop-loss' mechanic without arbitrary price-based stops. No explicit stop_loss_pct or lookback parameters provided by author, so momentum crossover timing is used as natural exit trigger. Suitable for trending regimes where momentum reversals are reliable.

source_rule_text: Exit a momentum position when a new entry signal in the opposite direction occurs, treating the reversal signal as a dynamic stop-loss mechanism.
